title: Connect an MCP Server to Your Agent
lede: Plug an entire toolbox into your Smart Agent in a couple of clicks. Attach an MCP server and your agent instantly discovers every tool it exposes — and can call them live, mid-conversation.

steps.title: What MCP Does for Your Agent
steps.content:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that lets an AI agent connect to an external server full of tools and resources — weather lookups, order systems, internal APIs, automation flows — without you wiring each tool by hand. When you attach an MCP server to a Smart Agent, the agent automatically discovers every tool the server exposes and can call any of them during a live call or chat.

There are two related pages in iMash — this guide covers the second one:

  • Registering MCP servers — building your own MCP server from automation flows in Automation. That's covered in MCP Servers.
  • Attaching an MCP server to an agent — connecting a registered (or external) server to a specific Smart Agent so its tools become part of that agent's skill set. That's this page.
💡 You don't need a registered server to follow along — the agent editor also accepts any external MCP server by URL, which you'll see in the "Add a Custom MCP Server" step below.

steps.title: Open Your Agent
steps.content:
1. In the left sidebar, under Intelligence Center, click Smart Agents.
  2. Click the name of the agent you want to supercharge. The agent editor opens.

If you don't have an agent yet, click Create New Agent first — see Create Your First Agent.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agent names as links
images.caption: Smart Agents list — click an agent's name to open the editor.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agent names as links
images.caption: Smart Agents list — click an agent's name to open the editor.

steps.title: Open the Tools / MCP Panel
steps.content:
Make sure the editor is in Form view (the Form / Node toggle sits at the top of the editor).

  1. Under the system prompt, click the Tools / MCP button. The same panel also opens from the Tools / MCP row in the right-hand column.

The number in parentheses on the button counts everything already attached — prebuilt tools, custom tools, and MCP servers.

images.alt: Agent editor form view with the Tools / MCP button under the prompt
images.caption: The Tools / MCP button sits right under the system prompt in Form view.

images.alt: Agent editor form view with the Tools / MCP button under the prompt
images.caption: The Tools / MCP button sits right under the system prompt in Form view.

steps.title: Meet the Tools & MCP Panel
steps.content:
The Tools & MCP panel is your agent's capability store. It has two halves:

  • Left rail — everything you can attach, in three sections: Predefined (built-in actions like End Call and SIP Transfer), Custom (your own HTTP/API tools), and MCP Servers at the bottom.
  • Right panel — everything already attached to this agent, newest first, each item tagged by type (PREBUILT, CUSTOM, MCP).
  1. Scroll the left rail all the way down to the MCP Servers section.
💡 Changes you make in this panel save immediately — there's no separate publish step. Close just dismisses the panel.

images.alt: Tools & MCP panel with the rail of available items on the left and attached items on the right
images.caption: Tools & MCP — available items on the left, attached items on the right.

images.alt: Tools & MCP panel with the rail of available items on the left and attached items on the right
images.caption: Tools & MCP — available items on the left, attached items on the right.

steps.title: Attach One of Your MCP Servers
steps.content:
Every MCP server you've registered in Automation → MCP shows up in the MCP Servers section automatically.

  1. Click a server in the list (the + on the right confirms it's attachable). It attaches instantly and its tag flips to Attached.
  2. You can attach multiple MCP servers to the same agent — each becomes its own card in the right panel.

Seeing "No built-in MCP servers configured" like in the screenshot? You haven't registered any servers yet. Click Set one up (it takes you to Automation), or continue with a custom URL in the next step. Registering your own server is covered in MCP Servers.

images.alt: MCP Servers section at the bottom of the rail with the Add custom MCP server button
images.caption: The MCP Servers section — registered servers list here; below it, Add custom MCP server.

images.alt: MCP Servers section at the bottom of the rail with the Add custom MCP server button
images.caption: The MCP Servers section — registered servers list here; below it, Add custom MCP server.

steps.title: Add a Custom MCP Server (Any URL)
steps.content:
Your agent isn't limited to servers built in iMash — it can connect to any MCP server on the internet.

  1. In the MCP Servers section, click Add custom MCP server.
  2. Fill in the dialog:
    • Name — a label you'll recognize later (e.g. "Weather MCP").
    • Server URL — the server's endpoint. URLs ending in /sse use SSE transport; /mcp use streamable HTTP — the right transport is auto-detected, so just paste the URL your provider gives you.
    • Auth header (optional) — if the server requires authentication, enter the header name (defaults to Authorization) and its value (e.g. Bearer <token>). The value is masked as you type.
  3. Click Attach.
⚠️ Only attach MCP servers you trust. The agent will happily call any tool the server exposes — a malicious server could feed it bad data or instructions.

images.alt: Add custom MCP server dialog with Name, Server URL and Auth header fields
images.caption: Add custom MCP server — name, URL, and an optional auth header.

images.alt: Add custom MCP server dialog with Name, Server URL and Auth header fields
images.caption: Add custom MCP server — name, URL, and an optional auth header.

steps.title: Review the Connection — Tool Discovery Is Automatic
steps.content:
Every attached server appears as a card in the right panel. This is the magic of MCP: you never map individual tools. At the start of each conversation the agent connects to the server, discovers whatever tools and resources it currently exposes, and can call them on demand. Add a new tool on the server side and your agent picks it up on the next call — no edits needed in iMash.

  1. Click a card's header to expand it. Custom servers show their URL and whether an auth header is set; built-in servers show a Manage MCP servers link back to Automation.
  2. To remove a server, click the trash icon on its card and confirm with Detach. Detaching only unlinks it from this agent — the server itself keeps existing.

images.alt: Attached MCP server card expanded showing the connection summary and server URL
images.caption: An attached custom MCP card — its tools and resources are available during conversations.

images.alt: Attached MCP server card expanded showing the connection summary and server URL
images.caption: An attached custom MCP card — its tools and resources are available during conversations.

steps.title: Advanced: Give an MCP Server to One Task Only (Node View)
steps.content:
Attaching from the Tools & MCP panel gives the server to your main agent. If you build multi-step agents with Tasks, you can also scope an MCP server to a single Task so only that part of the conversation can use it.

  1. At the top of the editor, switch the toggle from Form to Node.
  2. On the right-hand rail, click the Components (grid) icon and search for MCP. You'll find two draggable nodes under Tools:
    • MCP Server — attach one of your registered servers.
    • Custom MCP — attach an external server by URL.
  3. Drag one onto the canvas, pick the server (or enter the URL) in its side panel, then wire the node into the Task's Tools handle.

New to Tasks? Start with Sub Agents, Tasks & Handoffs.

images.alt: Node view Components palette filtered to MCP showing MCP Server and Custom MCP nodes
images.caption: In Node view, the Components palette has MCP Server and Custom MCP nodes under Tools.

images.alt: Node view Components palette filtered to MCP showing MCP Server and Custom MCP nodes
images.caption: In Node view, the Components palette has MCP Server and Custom MCP nodes under Tools.

steps.title: Test It Live
steps.content:
Time for the payoff — watch your agent use tools it was never explicitly taught.

  1. In the editor's top bar, click Test Agent, then Connect in the test panel.
  2. Ask something only the MCP server can answer — for example "What's the weather in Paris right now?" if you attached a weather server.
  3. Confirm the agent calls the tool and answers with real data. For servers built from iMash flows, each invocation (with its inputs and outputs) is also logged in Automation, so you can audit exactly what ran.
💡 If the agent doesn't reach for the tool, mention the capability in its system prompt (e.g. "You can look up live weather when asked") — a small hint dramatically improves tool usage.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Connect an MCP Server to Your Agent | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Attach an MCP server to your iMash Smart Agent: pick a registered server or connect any external MCP by URL, let the agent auto-discover its tools, scope servers to Tasks, and test live.
